summary: Low-risk, well-contained change. The architecture already has the primary plumbing: reduced-motion is detected in signals.ts, mapped to motionAmplitude in mappings.ts, and consumed in particleField.ts. The remaining work is extending the dampening to secondary motion sources (bass boost, treble shimmer/jitter, pointer disturbance, sparkle phase) and potentially attenuating evolution drift rates.

affected_files.change: Widen motionPref() or add companion helpers to also attenuate bassEnergy, trebleEnergy, and pointerDisturbance when reduced-motion is active. Alternatively, keep motionAmplitude as the single knob and let consumers apply it more broadly.
Apply motionAmplitude to bassBoost multiplier, treble shimmer/jitter, sparkle phase animation speed, and pointer disturbance push strength — not just base speed. This is the main behavioral change.
Optionally scale evolution drift amplitude by motionAmplitude so hue/cadence/saturation shifts are gentler under reduced-motion. Currently motionAmplitude passes through unchanged.
Already handles UI transitions via @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce). No change needed unless new UI animations are added.
No change needed — prefersReducedMotion detection is already implemented.

dependency_notes: US-004 (signals detection) and US-009 (visual mappings) are already implemented. motionAmplitude pipeline exists end-to-end.
existing_patterns_reused: motionAmplitude multiplier pattern already flows from signals -> mappings -> render loop
VisualParams interface already declares motionAmplitude with correct semantics
CSS media query pattern for UI elements already in place
test_considerations: Unit test motionPref() returns 0.2 when prefersReducedMotion is true
Unit test that particleField speed, bassBoost, shimmer, and jitter are all attenuated when motionAmplitude < 1
Visual smoke test: scene still renders and retains color/structure identity under reduced-motion
Verify no motion sources bypass the motionAmplitude gate
implementation_notes: The simplest approach: keep motionAmplitude as the single scalar knob and apply it multiplicatively to all secondary motion sources in particleField.draw() (bassBoost, shimmer, jitter, sparkle speed, pointer push). This preserves the existing architecture without adding new params or interfaces. For evolution.ts, pass motionAmplitude through to scale drift amplitudes. No new files, interfaces, or patterns needed.
